1	"""The unified model interface: ``load`` a model and run capabilities on it.
2	
3	This is the only module that ties the others together. ``protlms.load(name)``
4	returns a :class:`Model` whose ``embed``/``likelihood`` methods validate the
5	request against the model's manifest, stage inputs, drive the runner, and parse
6	the outputs into Python objects.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import logging
12	import os
13	import tempfile
14	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
15	from pathlib import Path
16	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
17	
18	from protlms.chunking import run_chunked
19	from protlms.contract import (
20	    Capability,
21	    Manifest,
22	    Result,
23	    check_contract_compatibility,
24	    parse_container_error,
25	)
26	from protlms.exceptions import (
27	    CapabilityNotSupportedError,
28	    ContainerExecutionError,
29	    InvalidRequestError,
30	)
31	from protlms.io import (
32	    check_csv_has_columns,
33	    load_per_residue_embeddings,
34	    load_pooled_embeddings,
35	    read_fasta,
36	    read_generated,
37	    read_likelihoods,
38	    read_result,
39	    read_variant_scores,
40	    stage_file,
41	    stage_inputs,
42	)
43	from protlms.registry import ModelEntry, Registry
44	from protlms.runner import Runner, RunSpec, SubprocessDockerRunner, ensure_image
45	
46	if TYPE_CHECKING:
47	    from collections.abc import Sequence
48	
49	    import numpy as np
50	
51	    from protlms.io import FastaRecord
52	
53	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
54	
55	_STDERR_TAIL_LINES = 50
56	
57	
58	@dataclass
59	class EmbeddingResult:
60	    """Handle to the outputs of an ``embed`` run (arrays loaded lazily)."""
61	
62	    result: Result
63	    output_dir: Path
64	    pooling: str
65	    _keepalive: tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None = field(default=None, repr=False)
66	
67	    def pooled(self) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
68	        """Return pooled embeddings keyed by record id (shape ``(embedding_dim,)``)."""
69	        return load_pooled_embeddings(self.output_dir, self.result)
70	
71	    def per_residue(self) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
72	        """Return per-residue embeddings keyed by record id (shape ``(L, embedding_dim)``)."""
73	        return load_per_residue_embeddings(self.output_dir, self.result)
74	
75	
76	@dataclass
77	class LikelihoodResult:
78	    """Handle to the outputs of a ``likelihood`` run (CSV parsed lazily)."""
79	
80	    result: Result
81	    output_dir: Path
82	    _keepalive: tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None = field(default=None, repr=False)
83	
84	    def rows(self) -> list[dict[str, str | int | float | None]]:
85	        """Return one row per record with likelihood/perplexity columns."""
86	        return read_likelihoods(self.output_dir, self.result)
87	
88	
89	@dataclass
90	class ScoreResult:
91	    """Handle to the outputs of a ``score`` run (CSV parsed lazily)."""
92	
93	    result: Result
94	    output_dir: Path
95	    method: str
96	    _keepalive: tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None = field(default=None, repr=False)
97	
98	    def rows(self) -> list[dict[str, str | int | float | None]]:
99	        """Return one row per variant: variant_id, mutant, n_mutations, score."""
100	        return read_variant_scores(self.output_dir, self.result)
101	
102	
103	@dataclass
104	class GenerationResult:
105	    """Handle to the outputs of a ``generate`` run (FASTA parsed lazily)."""
106	
107	    result: Result
108	    output_dir: Path
109	    _keepalive: tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None = field(default=None, repr=False)
110	
111	    def sequences(self) -> list[FastaRecord]:
112	        """Return the generated sequences (headers ``{prompt_id}__sample{k}``)."""
113	        return read_generated(self.output_dir, self.result)
114	
115	
116	class Model:
117	    """A loaded model exposing the unified inference capabilities."""
118	
119	    def __init__(
120	        self, entry: ModelEntry, runner: Runner, manifest: Manifest, image_ref: str
121	    ) -> None:
122	        self._entry = entry
123	        self._runner = runner
124	        self._manifest = manifest
125	        self._image_ref = image_ref
126	
127	    @property
128	    def manifest(self) -> Manifest:
129	        """The model's declared manifest."""
130	        return self._manifest
131	
132	    @property
133	    def name(self) -> str:
134	        """The model's canonical name."""
135	        return self._manifest.name
136	
137	    def embed(
138	        self,
139	        fasta: str | Path,
140	        *,
141	        pooling: str = "mean",
142	        layers: Sequence[int] = (-1,),
143	        output_dir: Path | None = None,
144	        use_gpu: bool = False,
145	        batch_size: int | None = None,
146	        chunk_size: int | None = None,
147	    ) -> EmbeddingResult:
148	        """Compute embeddings for the sequences in ``fasta``.
149	
150	        Args:
151	            fasta: Path to the input FASTA file.
152	            pooling: One of the model's supported pooling modes (``mean``/``cls``
153	                produce pooled vectors; ``none`` produces per-residue arrays).
154	            layers: Transformer layer indices to read (negative indexing allowed).
155	            output_dir: Where to write outputs; a temporary directory if ``None``.
156	            use_gpu: Request all GPUs for the container run.
157	            batch_size: Override the model's default batch size.
158	            chunk_size: If set, split the input into runs of at most this many records and
159	                merge the outputs (resumable into a persistent output_dir).
160	
161	        Raises:
162	            CapabilityNotSupportedError: If the model does not support embedding.
163	            InvalidRequestError: If ``pooling`` is unsupported or the input is empty.
164	            ContainerExecutionError: If the container run fails.
165	        """
166	        self._require_capability(Capability.EMBED)
167	        self._require_pooling(pooling)
168	        records = self._read_records(fasta)
169	        extra = ["--pooling", pooling, "--layers", ",".join(str(x) for x in layers)]
170	        if batch_size is not None:
171	            extra += ["--batch-size", str(batch_size)]
172	        if chunk_size is not None:
173	            result, out_dir, keep = self._run_chunked(
174	                Capability.EMBED, records, extra, output_dir, use_gpu, chunk_size
175	            )
176	        else:
177	            result, out_dir, keep = self._run(
178	                Capability.EMBED, stage_inputs(records), extra, output_dir, use_gpu
179	            )
180	        return EmbeddingResult(result=result, output_dir=out_dir, pooling=pooling, _keepalive=keep)
181	
182	    def likelihood(
183	        self,
184	        fasta: str | Path,
185	        *,
186	        output_dir: Path | None = None,
187	        use_gpu: bool = False,
188	        batch_size: int | None = None,
189	        chunk_size: int | None = None,
190	    ) -> LikelihoodResult:
191	        """Compute per-sequence log-likelihoods.
192	
193	        The scoring method depends on the model: masked-marginal for masked LMs,
194	        causal (left-to-right) for autoregressive LMs. The method used is recorded
195	        in ``result.json`` under ``params.likelihood_method``.
196	
197	        Args:
198	            fasta: Path to the input FASTA file.
199	            output_dir: Where to write outputs; a temporary directory if ``None``.
200	            use_gpu: Request all GPUs for the container run.
201	            batch_size: Override the model's default batch size.
202	            chunk_size: If set, split the input into runs of at most this many records and
203	                merge the outputs (resumable into a persistent output_dir).
204	
205	        Raises:
206	            CapabilityNotSupportedError: If the model does not support likelihoods.
207	            InvalidRequestError: If the input is empty.
208	            ContainerExecutionError: If the container run fails.
209	        """
210	        self._require_capability(Capability.LIKELIHOOD)
211	        records = self._read_records(fasta)
212	        extra = ["--batch-size", str(batch_size)] if batch_size is not None else []
213	        if chunk_size is not None:
214	            result, out_dir, keep = self._run_chunked(
215	                Capability.LIKELIHOOD, records, extra, output_dir, use_gpu, chunk_size
216	            )
217	        else:
218	            result, out_dir, keep = self._run(
219	                Capability.LIKELIHOOD, stage_inputs(records), extra, output_dir, use_gpu
220	            )
221	        return LikelihoodResult(result=result, output_dir=out_dir, _keepalive=keep)
222	
223	    def score(
224	        self,
225	        variants_csv: str | Path,
226	        *,
227	        method: str = "masked-marginal",
228	        output_dir: Path | None = None,
229	        use_gpu: bool = False,
230	        batch_size: int | None = None,
231	    ) -> ScoreResult:
232	        """Score sequence variants for effect.
233	
234	        Args:
235	            variants_csv: CSV with columns ``variant_id, wt_sequence, mutant``.
236	            method: ``"masked-marginal"`` (default) or ``"wt-marginal"``.
237	            output_dir: Where to write outputs; a temporary directory if ``None``.
238	            use_gpu: Request all GPUs for the container run.
239	            batch_size: Override the model's default batch size.
240	
241	        Raises:
242	            CapabilityNotSupportedError: If the model does not support scoring.
243	            InvalidRequestError: If ``method`` is invalid or the CSV lacks columns.
244	            ContainerExecutionError: If the container run fails.
245	        """
246	        self._require_capability(Capability.SCORE)
247	        if method not in ("masked-marginal", "wt-marginal"):
248	            raise InvalidRequestError(
249	                f"unsupported scoring method {method!r}; choose 'masked-marginal' or 'wt-marginal'"
250	            )
251	        path = Path(variants_csv)
252	        check_csv_has_columns(path, ("variant_id", "wt_sequence", "mutant"))
253	        extra = ["--method", method]
254	        if batch_size is not None:
255	            extra += ["--batch-size", str(batch_size)]
256	        result, out_dir, keep = self._run(
257	            Capability.SCORE, stage_file(path, "variants.csv"), extra, output_dir, use_gpu
258	        )
259	        return ScoreResult(result=result, output_dir=out_dir, method=method, _keepalive=keep)
260	
261	    def generate(
262	        self,
263	        prompts_fasta: str | Path,
264	        *,
265	        num_samples: int = 1,
266	        temperature: float = 1.0,
267	        top_p: float = 1.0,
268	        max_length: int | None = None,
269	        seed: int | None = None,
270	        output_dir: Path | None = None,
271	        use_gpu: bool = False,
272	        batch_size: int | None = None,
273	        chunk_size: int | None = None,
274	    ) -> GenerationResult:
275	        """Sample sequences from an autoregressive model.
276	
277	        Args:
278	            prompts_fasta: FASTA of prompt prefixes; an empty sequence means
279	                unconditional sampling. At least one record is required.
280	            num_samples: Samples to draw per prompt.
281	            temperature: Sampling temperature.
282	            top_p: Nucleus-sampling probability mass.
283	            max_length: Maximum sequence length (model default if ``None``).
284	            seed: Random seed for reproducible sampling.
285	            output_dir: Where to write outputs; a temporary directory if ``None``.
286	            use_gpu: Request all GPUs for the container run.
287	            batch_size: Override the model's default batch size.
288	            chunk_size: If set, split the input into runs of at most this many records and
289	                merge the outputs (resumable into a persistent output_dir).
290	
291	        Raises:
292	            CapabilityNotSupportedError: If the model does not support generation.
293	            InvalidRequestError: If the prompts file contains no records.
294	            ContainerExecutionError: If the container run fails.
295	        """
296	        self._require_capability(Capability.GENERATE)
297	        records = self._read_records(prompts_fasta)
298	        extra = [
299	            "--num-samples",
300	            str(num_samples),
301	            "--temperature",
302	            str(temperature),
303	            "--top-p",
304	            str(top_p),
305	        ]
306	        if max_length is not None:
307	            extra += ["--max-length", str(max_length)]
308	        if seed is not None:
309	            extra += ["--seed", str(seed)]
310	        if batch_size is not None:
311	            extra += ["--batch-size", str(batch_size)]
312	        if chunk_size is not None:
313	            result, out_dir, keep = self._run_chunked(
314	                Capability.GENERATE, records, extra, output_dir, use_gpu, chunk_size
315	            )
316	        else:
317	            result, out_dir, keep = self._run(
318	                Capability.GENERATE, stage_inputs(records), extra, output_dir, use_gpu
319	            )
320	        return GenerationResult(result=result, output_dir=out_dir, _keepalive=keep)
321	
322	    # --- internals ---------------------------------------------------------
323	
324	    def _require_capability(self, capability: Capability) -> None:
325	        if capability not in self._manifest.capabilities:
326	            raise CapabilityNotSupportedError(
327	                f"model {self._manifest.name!r} does not support {capability.value!r}; "
328	                f"supported: {[c.value for c in self._manifest.capabilities]}"
329	            )
330	
331	    def _require_pooling(self, pooling: str) -> None:
332	        supported = {m.value for m in self._manifest.pooling_modes}
333	        if pooling not in supported:
334	            raise InvalidRequestError(
335	                f"unsupported pooling {pooling!r}; model supports {sorted(supported)}"
336	            )
337	
338	    def _read_records(self, fasta: str | Path) -> list[FastaRecord]:
339	        records = read_fasta(Path(fasta))
340	        if not records:
341	            raise InvalidRequestError(f"input FASTA {fasta} contains no records")
342	        too_long = [r.id for r in records if len(r.sequence) > self._manifest.max_sequence_length]
343	        if too_long:
344	            logger.warning(
345	                "%d sequence(s) exceed max_sequence_length=%d and will be truncated by the "
346	                "container: %s",
347	                len(too_long),
348	                self._manifest.max_sequence_length,
349	                too_long[:5],
350	            )
351	        return records
352	
353	    def _run_into_dir(
354	        self,
355	        capability: Capability,
356	        staging,  # contextmanager[StagedInput]
357	        extra_args: list[str],
358	        out_dir: Path,
359	        use_gpu: bool,
360	    ) -> Result:
361	        """Run one container job into ``out_dir`` and return its parsed Result."""
362	        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
363	        with staging as staged:
364	            command = [
365	                capability.value,
366	                "--input",
367	                staged.container_input_path,
368	                "--output",
369	                "/out",
370	                *extra_args,
371	            ]
372	            spec = RunSpec(
373	                image=self._image_ref,
374	                command=command,
375	                input_dir=staged.input_dir,
376	                output_dir=out_dir,
377	                use_gpu=use_gpu,
378	            )
379	            run_result = self._runner.run(spec)
380	        if run_result.exit_code != 0:
381	            self._raise_container_error(run_result)
382	        return read_result(out_dir)
383	
384	    def _run(
385	        self,
386	        capability: Capability,
387	        staging,  # contextmanager[StagedInput]
388	        extra_args: list[str],
389	        output_dir: Path | None,
390	        use_gpu: bool,
391	    ) -> tuple[Result, Path, tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None]:
392	        out_dir, keep = self._resolve_output_dir(output_dir)
393	        result = self._run_into_dir(capability, staging, extra_args, out_dir, use_gpu)
394	        return result, out_dir, keep
395	
396	    def _run_chunked(
397	        self,
398	        capability: Capability,
399	        records: list[FastaRecord],
400	        extra_args: list[str],
401	        output_dir: Path | None,
402	        use_gpu: bool,
403	        chunk_size: int,
404	    ) -> tuple[Result, Path, tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None]:
405	        out_dir, keep = self._resolve_output_dir(output_dir)
406	
407	        def run_chunk(chunk: list[FastaRecord], chunk_dir: Path) -> Result:
408	            return self._run_into_dir(
409	                capability, stage_inputs(chunk), extra_args, chunk_dir, use_gpu
410	            )
411	
412	        result = run_chunked(
413	            capability=capability.value,
414	            records=records,
415	            chunk_size=chunk_size,
416	            output_dir=out_dir,
417	            run_chunk=run_chunk,
418	        )
419	        return result, out_dir, keep
420	
421	    @staticmethod
422	    def _resolve_output_dir(
423	        output_dir: Path | None,
424	    ) -> tuple[Path, tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None]:
425	        if output_dir is not None:
426	            out = Path(output_dir).resolve()
427	            out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
428	            return out, None
429	        keep = tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="protlms-out-")
430	        return Path(keep.name).resolve(), keep
431	
432	    @staticmethod
433	    def _raise_container_error(run_result) -> None:  # noqa: ANN001
434	        tail = "\n".join(run_result.stderr.splitlines()[-_STDERR_TAIL_LINES:])
435	        err = parse_container_error(run_result.stderr)
436	        if err is not None:
437	            raise ContainerExecutionError(
438	                err.message,
439	                error_type=err.error_type,
440	                details=err.details,
441	                exit_code=run_result.exit_code,
442	                stderr_tail=tail,
443	            )
444	        raise ContainerExecutionError(
445	            f"container exited with code {run_result.exit_code}",
446	            exit_code=run_result.exit_code,
447	            stderr_tail=tail,
448	        )
449	
450	
451	def _resolve_allow_pull(allow_pull: bool | None) -> bool:
452	    """Resolve pull policy: explicit arg wins, else consult ``PROTLMS_NO_PULL``."""
453	    if allow_pull is not None:
454	        return allow_pull
455	    no_pull = os.environ.get("PROTLMS_NO_PULL", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes"}
456	    return not no_pull
457	
458	
459	def load(
460	    name: str,
461	    *,
462	    runner: Runner | None = None,
463	    registry: Registry | None = None,
464	    allow_pull: bool | None = None,
465	) -> Model:
466	    """Resolve a model name and return a ready-to-use :class:`Model`.
467	
468	    Resolves the name against the registry, ensures the pinned image is present
469	    locally (pulling it when permitted), reads the image's manifest, checks
470	    contract compatibility, and constructs the model.
471	
472	    Args:
473	        name: A model name or alias known to the registry.
474	        runner: The container runner (defaults to a local docker subprocess runner).
475	        registry: The model registry (defaults to the packaged registry).
476	        allow_pull: Whether to pull a missing image. ``None`` (default) consults
477	            the ``PROTLMS_NO_PULL`` environment variable.
478	
479	    Raises:
480	        ModelNotFoundError: If the name is unknown.
481	        ImageNotFoundError: If the image is absent and pulling is disabled.
482	        ImagePullError: If the image must be pulled and the pull fails.
483	        ContractVersionError: If the image's contract major version mismatches.
484	    """
485	    runner = runner or SubprocessDockerRunner()
486	    registry = registry or Registry.load()
487	    entry = registry.resolve(name)
488	    ref = entry.pinned_ref()
489	    ensure_image(runner, ref, allow_pull=_resolve_allow_pull(allow_pull), model_name=name)
490	    manifest = Manifest.model_validate_json(runner.manifest(ref))
491	    check_contract_compatibility(manifest.contract_version)
492	    return Model(entry, runner, manifest, image_ref=ref)
493

file.content:
"""The unified model interface: ``load`` a model and run capabilities on it.

This is the only module that ties the others together. ``protlms.load(name)``
returns a :class:`Model` whose ``embed``/``likelihood`` methods validate the
request against the model's manifest, stage inputs, drive the runner, and parse
the outputs into Python objects.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import os
import tempfile
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

from protlms.chunking import run_chunked
from protlms.contract import (
    Capability,
    Manifest,
    Result,
    check_contract_compatibility,
    parse_container_error,
)
from protlms.exceptions import (
    CapabilityNotSupportedError,
    ContainerExecutionError,
    InvalidRequestError,
)
from protlms.io import (
    check_csv_has_columns,
    load_per_residue_embeddings,
    load_pooled_embeddings,
    read_fasta,
    read_generated,
    read_likelihoods,
    read_result,
    read_variant_scores,
    stage_file,
    stage_inputs,
)
from protlms.registry import ModelEntry, Registry
from protlms.runner import Runner, RunSpec, SubprocessDockerRunner, ensure_image

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from collections.abc import Sequence

    import numpy as np

    from protlms.io import FastaRecord

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_STDERR_TAIL_LINES = 50


@dataclass
class EmbeddingResult:
    """Handle to the outputs of an ``embed`` run (arrays loaded lazily)."""

    result: Result
    output_dir: Path
    pooling: str
    _keepalive: tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None = field(default=None, repr=False)

    def pooled(self) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
        """Return pooled embeddings keyed by record id (shape ``(embedding_dim,)``)."""
        return load_pooled_embeddings(self.output_dir, self.result)

    def per_residue(self) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
        """Return per-residue embeddings keyed by record id (shape ``(L, embedding_dim)``)."""
        return load_per_residue_embeddings(self.output_dir, self.result)


@dataclass
class LikelihoodResult:
    """Handle to the outputs of a ``likelihood`` run (CSV parsed lazily)."""

    result: Result
    output_dir: Path
    _keepalive: tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None = field(default=None, repr=False)

    def rows(self) -> list[dict[str, str | int | float | None]]:
        """Return one row per record with likelihood/perplexity columns."""
        return read_likelihoods(self.output_dir, self.result)


@dataclass
class ScoreResult:
    """Handle to the outputs of a ``score`` run (CSV parsed lazily)."""

    result: Result
    output_dir: Path
    method: str
    _keepalive: tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None = field(default=None, repr=False)

    def rows(self) -> list[dict[str, str | int | float | None]]:
        """Return one row per variant: variant_id, mutant, n_mutations, score."""
        return read_variant_scores(self.output_dir, self.result)


@dataclass
class GenerationResult:
    """Handle to the outputs of a ``generate`` run (FASTA parsed lazily)."""

    result: Result
    output_dir: Path
    _keepalive: tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None = field(default=None, repr=False)

    def sequences(self) -> list[FastaRecord]:
        """Return the generated sequences (headers ``{prompt_id}__sample{k}``)."""
        return read_generated(self.output_dir, self.result)


class Model:
    """A loaded model exposing the unified inference capabilities."""

    def __init__(
        self, entry: ModelEntry, runner: Runner, manifest: Manifest, image_ref: str
    ) -> None:
        self._entry = entry
        self._runner = runner
        self._manifest = manifest
        self._image_ref = image_ref

    @property
    def manifest(self) -> Manifest:
        """The model's declared manifest."""
        return self._manifest

    @property
    def name(self) -> str:
        """The model's canonical name."""
        return self._manifest.name

    def embed(
        self,
        fasta: str | Path,
        *,
        pooling: str = "mean",
        layers: Sequence[int] = (-1,),
        output_dir: Path | None = None,
        use_gpu: bool = False,
        batch_size: int | None = None,
        chunk_size: int | None = None,
    ) -> EmbeddingResult:
        """Compute embeddings for the sequences in ``fasta``.

        Args:
            fasta: Path to the input FASTA file.
            pooling: One of the model's supported pooling modes (``mean``/``cls``
                produce pooled vectors; ``none`` produces per-residue arrays).
            layers: Transformer layer indices to read (negative indexing allowed).
            output_dir: Where to write outputs; a temporary directory if ``None``.
            use_gpu: Request all GPUs for the container run.
            batch_size: Override the model's default batch size.
            chunk_size: If set, split the input into runs of at most this many records and
                merge the outputs (resumable into a persistent output_dir).

        Raises:
            CapabilityNotSupportedError: If the model does not support embedding.
            InvalidRequestError: If ``pooling`` is unsupported or the input is empty.
            ContainerExecutionError: If the container run fails.
        """
        self._require_capability(Capability.EMBED)
        self._require_pooling(pooling)
        records = self._read_records(fasta)
        extra = ["--pooling", pooling, "--layers", ",".join(str(x) for x in layers)]
        if batch_size is not None:
            extra += ["--batch-size", str(batch_size)]
        if chunk_size is not None:
            result, out_dir, keep = self._run_chunked(
                Capability.EMBED, records, extra, output_dir, use_gpu, chunk_size
            )
        else:
            result, out_dir, keep = self._run(
                Capability.EMBED, stage_inputs(records), extra, output_dir, use_gpu
            )
        return EmbeddingResult(result=result, output_dir=out_dir, pooling=pooling, _keepalive=keep)

    def likelihood(
        self,
        fasta: str | Path,
        *,
        output_dir: Path | None = None,
        use_gpu: bool = False,
        batch_size: int | None = None,
        chunk_size: int | None = None,
    ) -> LikelihoodResult:
        """Compute per-sequence log-likelihoods.

        The scoring method depends on the model: masked-marginal for masked LMs,
        causal (left-to-right) for autoregressive LMs. The method used is recorded
        in ``result.json`` under ``params.likelihood_method``.

        Args:
            fasta: Path to the input FASTA file.
            output_dir: Where to write outputs; a temporary directory if ``None``.
            use_gpu: Request all GPUs for the container run.
            batch_size: Override the model's default batch size.
            chunk_size: If set, split the input into runs of at most this many records and
                merge the outputs (resumable into a persistent output_dir).

        Raises:
            CapabilityNotSupportedError: If the model does not support likelihoods.
            InvalidRequestError: If the input is empty.
            ContainerExecutionError: If the container run fails.
        """
        self._require_capability(Capability.LIKELIHOOD)
        records = self._read_records(fasta)
        extra = ["--batch-size", str(batch_size)] if batch_size is not None else []
        if chunk_size is not None:
            result, out_dir, keep = self._run_chunked(
                Capability.LIKELIHOOD, records, extra, output_dir, use_gpu, chunk_size
            )
        else:
            result, out_dir, keep = self._run(
                Capability.LIKELIHOOD, stage_inputs(records), extra, output_dir, use_gpu
            )
        return LikelihoodResult(result=result, output_dir=out_dir, _keepalive=keep)

    def score(
        self,
        variants_csv: str | Path,
        *,
        method: str = "masked-marginal",
        output_dir: Path | None = None,
        use_gpu: bool = False,
        batch_size: int | None = None,
    ) -> ScoreResult:
        """Score sequence variants for effect.

        Args:
            variants_csv: CSV with columns ``variant_id, wt_sequence, mutant``.
            method: ``"masked-marginal"`` (default) or ``"wt-marginal"``.
            output_dir: Where to write outputs; a temporary directory if ``None``.
            use_gpu: Request all GPUs for the container run.
            batch_size: Override the model's default batch size.

        Raises:
            CapabilityNotSupportedError: If the model does not support scoring.
            InvalidRequestError: If ``method`` is invalid or the CSV lacks columns.
            ContainerExecutionError: If the container run fails.
        """
        self._require_capability(Capability.SCORE)
        if method not in ("masked-marginal", "wt-marginal"):
            raise InvalidRequestError(
                f"unsupported scoring method {method!r}; choose 'masked-marginal' or 'wt-marginal'"
            )
        path = Path(variants_csv)
        check_csv_has_columns(path, ("variant_id", "wt_sequence", "mutant"))
        extra = ["--method", method]
        if batch_size is not None:
            extra += ["--batch-size", str(batch_size)]
        result, out_dir, keep = self._run(
            Capability.SCORE, stage_file(path, "variants.csv"), extra, output_dir, use_gpu
        )
        return ScoreResult(result=result, output_dir=out_dir, method=method, _keepalive=keep)

    def generate(
        self,
        prompts_fasta: str | Path,
        *,
        num_samples: int = 1,
        temperature: float = 1.0,
        top_p: float = 1.0,
        max_length: int | None = None,
        seed: int | None = None,
        output_dir: Path | None = None,
        use_gpu: bool = False,
        batch_size: int | None = None,
        chunk_size: int | None = None,
    ) -> GenerationResult:
        """Sample sequences from an autoregressive model.

        Args:
            prompts_fasta: FASTA of prompt prefixes; an empty sequence means
                unconditional sampling. At least one record is required.
            num_samples: Samples to draw per prompt.
            temperature: Sampling temperature.
            top_p: Nucleus-sampling probability mass.
            max_length: Maximum sequence length (model default if ``None``).
            seed: Random seed for reproducible sampling.
            output_dir: Where to write outputs; a temporary directory if ``None``.
            use_gpu: Request all GPUs for the container run.
            batch_size: Override the model's default batch size.
            chunk_size: If set, split the input into runs of at most this many records and
                merge the outputs (resumable into a persistent output_dir).

        Raises:
            CapabilityNotSupportedError: If the model does not support generation.
            InvalidRequestError: If the prompts file contains no records.
            ContainerExecutionError: If the container run fails.
        """
        self._require_capability(Capability.GENERATE)
        records = self._read_records(prompts_fasta)
        extra = [
            "--num-samples",
            str(num_samples),
            "--temperature",
            str(temperature),
            "--top-p",
            str(top_p),
        ]
        if max_length is not None:
            extra += ["--max-length", str(max_length)]
        if seed is not None:
            extra += ["--seed", str(seed)]
        if batch_size is not None:
            extra += ["--batch-size", str(batch_size)]
        if chunk_size is not None:
            result, out_dir, keep = self._run_chunked(
                Capability.GENERATE, records, extra, output_dir, use_gpu, chunk_size
            )
        else:
            result, out_dir, keep = self._run(
                Capability.GENERATE, stage_inputs(records), extra, output_dir, use_gpu
            )
        return GenerationResult(result=result, output_dir=out_dir, _keepalive=keep)

    # --- internals ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _require_capability(self, capability: Capability) -> None:
        if capability not in self._manifest.capabilities:
            raise CapabilityNotSupportedError(
                f"model {self._manifest.name!r} does not support {capability.value!r}; "
                f"supported: {[c.value for c in self._manifest.capabilities]}"
            )

    def _require_pooling(self, pooling: str) -> None:
        supported = {m.value for m in self._manifest.pooling_modes}
        if pooling not in supported:
            raise InvalidRequestError(
                f"unsupported pooling {pooling!r}; model supports {sorted(supported)}"
            )

    def _read_records(self, fasta: str | Path) -> list[FastaRecord]:
        records = read_fasta(Path(fasta))
        if not records:
            raise InvalidRequestError(f"input FASTA {fasta} contains no records")
        too_long = [r.id for r in records if len(r.sequence) > self._manifest.max_sequence_length]
        if too_long:
            logger.warning(
                "%d sequence(s) exceed max_sequence_length=%d and will be truncated by the "
                "container: %s",
                len(too_long),
                self._manifest.max_sequence_length,
                too_long[:5],
            )
        return records

    def _run_into_dir(
        self,
        capability: Capability,
        staging,  # contextmanager[StagedInput]
        extra_args: list[str],
        out_dir: Path,
        use_gpu: bool,
    ) -> Result:
        """Run one container job into ``out_dir`` and return its parsed Result."""
        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        with staging as staged:
            command = [
                capability.value,
                "--input",
                staged.container_input_path,
                "--output",
                "/out",
                *extra_args,
            ]
            spec = RunSpec(
                image=self._image_ref,
                command=command,
                input_dir=staged.input_dir,
                output_dir=out_dir,
                use_gpu=use_gpu,
            )
            run_result = self._runner.run(spec)
        if run_result.exit_code != 0:
            self._raise_container_error(run_result)
        return read_result(out_dir)

    def _run(
        self,
        capability: Capability,
        staging,  # contextmanager[StagedInput]
        extra_args: list[str],
        output_dir: Path | None,
        use_gpu: bool,
    ) -> tuple[Result, Path, tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None]:
        out_dir, keep = self._resolve_output_dir(output_dir)
        result = self._run_into_dir(capability, staging, extra_args, out_dir, use_gpu)
        return result, out_dir, keep

    def _run_chunked(
        self,
        capability: Capability,
        records: list[FastaRecord],
        extra_args: list[str],
        output_dir: Path | None,
        use_gpu: bool,
        chunk_size: int,
    ) -> tuple[Result, Path, tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None]:
        out_dir, keep = self._resolve_output_dir(output_dir)

        def run_chunk(chunk: list[FastaRecord], chunk_dir: Path) -> Result:
            return self._run_into_dir(
                capability, stage_inputs(chunk), extra_args, chunk_dir, use_gpu
            )

        result = run_chunked(
            capability=capability.value,
            records=records,
            chunk_size=chunk_size,
            output_dir=out_dir,
            run_chunk=run_chunk,
        )
        return result, out_dir, keep

    @staticmethod
    def _resolve_output_dir(
        output_dir: Path | None,
    ) -> tuple[Path, tempfile.TemporaryDirectory | None]:
        if output_dir is not None:
            out = Path(output_dir).resolve()
            out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            return out, None
        keep = tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="protlms-out-")
        return Path(keep.name).resolve(), keep

    @staticmethod
    def _raise_container_error(run_result) -> None:  # noqa: ANN001
        tail = "\n".join(run_result.stderr.splitlines()[-_STDERR_TAIL_LINES:])
        err = parse_container_error(run_result.stderr)
        if err is not None:
            raise ContainerExecutionError(
                err.message,
                error_type=err.error_type,
                details=err.details,
                exit_code=run_result.exit_code,
                stderr_tail=tail,
            )
        raise ContainerExecutionError(
            f"container exited with code {run_result.exit_code}",
            exit_code=run_result.exit_code,
            stderr_tail=tail,
        )


def _resolve_allow_pull(allow_pull: bool | None) -> bool:
    """Resolve pull policy: explicit arg wins, else consult ``PROTLMS_NO_PULL``."""
    if allow_pull is not None:
        return allow_pull
    no_pull = os.environ.get("PROTLMS_NO_PULL", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes"}
    return not no_pull


def load(
    name: str,
    *,
    runner: Runner | None = None,
    registry: Registry | None = None,
    allow_pull: bool | None = None,
) -> Model:
    """Resolve a model name and return a ready-to-use :class:`Model`.

    Resolves the name against the registry, ensures the pinned image is present
    locally (pulling it when permitted), reads the image's manifest, checks
    contract compatibility, and constructs the model.

    Args:
        name: A model name or alias known to the registry.
        runner: The container runner (defaults to a local docker subprocess runner).
        registry: The model registry (defaults to the packaged registry).
        allow_pull: Whether to pull a missing image. ``None`` (default) consults
            the ``PROTLMS_NO_PULL`` environment variable.

    Raises:
        ModelNotFoundError: If the name is unknown.
        ImageNotFoundError: If the image is absent and pulling is disabled.
        ImagePullError: If the image must be pulled and the pull fails.
        ContractVersionError: If the image's contract major version mismatches.
    """
    runner = runner or SubprocessDockerRunner()
    registry = registry or Registry.load()
    entry = registry.resolve(name)
    ref = entry.pinned_ref()
    ensure_image(runner, ref, allow_pull=_resolve_allow_pull(allow_pull), model_name=name)
    manifest = Manifest.model_validate_json(runner.manifest(ref))
    check_contract_compatibility(manifest.contract_version)
    return Model(entry, runner, manifest, image_ref=ref)

"""Input/output handling: FASTA parsing, input staging, and output parsing.

This module touches the filesystem but knows nothing about Docker. ``numpy`` is
imported only to load result arrays produced by containers.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import csv
import shutil
import tempfile
from contextlib import contextmanager
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

import numpy as np

from protlms.contract import ArtifactKind, Result
from protlms.exceptions import FastaError, InvalidRequestError, OutputParseError

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from collections.abc import Iterable, Iterator

#: The fixed filename a staged FASTA is normalized to inside the input mount.
STAGED_FASTA_NAME = "seqs.fasta"

#: Numeric columns in the likelihoods CSV and the type to coerce them to.
_LIKELIHOOD_COLUMN_TYPES: dict[str, type] = {
    "seq_len": int,
    "log_likelihood": float,
    "mean_log_likelihood": float,
    "perplexity": float,
}

#: Numeric columns in the variant scores CSV and the type to coerce them to.
_SCORE_COLUMN_TYPES: dict[str, type] = {"n_mutations": int, "score": float}


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class FastaRecord:
    """A single FASTA record.

    Attributes:
        id: The first whitespace-delimited token of the header.
        description: The full header line (without the leading ``>``).
        sequence: The (newline-joined, uppercased) residue sequence.
    """

    id: str
    description: str
    sequence: str


def read_fasta(path: Path) -> list[FastaRecord]:
    """Parse a FASTA file into records.

    Args:
        path: Path to a FASTA file.

    Returns:
        The records in file order (empty list for an empty file).

    Raises:
        FastaError: If sequence data appears before any header line.
    """
    records: list[FastaRecord] = []
    header: str | None = None
    chunks: list[str] = []

    def flush() -> None:
        if header is not None:
            record_id = header.split(maxsplit=1)[0] if header.split() else header
            records.append(FastaRecord(record_id, header, "".join(chunks).upper()))

    for raw in path.read_text().splitlines():
        line = raw.strip()
        if not line:
            continue
        if line.startswith(">"):
            flush()
            header = line[1:].strip()
            chunks = []
        else:
            if header is None:
                raise FastaError(f"sequence data before any '>' header in {path}")
            chunks.append(line)
    flush()
    return records


def write_fasta(records: Iterable[FastaRecord], path: Path) -> None:
    """Write records to ``path`` as FASTA, using only the record id as header."""
    lines = [f">{record.id}\n{record.sequence}\n" for record in records]
    path.write_text("".join(lines))


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class StagedInput:
    """The host input mount for one staged container run."""

    input_dir: Path
    input_filename: str = STAGED_FASTA_NAME

    @property
    def container_input_path(self) -> str:
        """The input path as seen inside the container (under ``/in``)."""
        return f"/in/{self.input_filename}"


@contextmanager
def stage_inputs(records: list[FastaRecord]) -> Iterator[StagedInput]:
    """Stage records into a temporary, read-only-bound input directory.

    Writes a normalized FASTA (id-only headers) into a temporary directory that
    is removed on context exit. The output directory is managed separately by
    the caller, since outputs must outlive the run.

    Args:
        records: The records to stage.

    Yields:
        A :class:`StagedInput` pointing at the host input directory.

    Raises:
        FastaError: If two records share the same id.
    """
    ids = [r.id for r in records]
    duplicates = {i for i in ids if ids.count(i) > 1}
    if duplicates:
        raise FastaError(f"duplicate record ids in input: {sorted(duplicates)}")

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="protlms-in-") as tmp:
        input_dir = Path(tmp)
        write_fasta(records, input_dir / STAGED_FASTA_NAME)
        yield StagedInput(input_dir=input_dir)


@contextmanager
def stage_file(src: Path, dest_name: str) -> Iterator[StagedInput]:
    """Stage an arbitrary input file into a temporary directory bound at /in.

    Args:
        src: The host file to stage.
        dest_name: The filename it should have inside the input directory.

    Yields:
        A :class:`StagedInput` pointing at the host input directory.
    """
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="protlms-in-") as tmp:
        input_dir = Path(tmp)
        shutil.copyfile(src, input_dir / dest_name)
        yield StagedInput(input_dir=input_dir, input_filename=dest_name)


def check_csv_has_columns(path: Path, required: Iterable[str]) -> None:
    """Validate that a CSV file's header contains all required columns.

    Raises:
        InvalidRequestError: If any required column is absent.
    """
    with path.open(newline="") as handle:
        header = next(csv.reader(handle), [])
    missing = [column for column in required if column not in header]
    if missing:
        raise InvalidRequestError(f"variants CSV {path} is missing column(s): {missing}")


def read_result(out_dir: Path) -> Result:
    """Load and parse ``result.json`` from a container's output directory.

    Args:
        out_dir: The host output directory the container wrote to.

    Returns:
        The parsed :class:`~protlms.contract.Result`.

    Raises:
        OutputParseError: If ``result.json`` is missing or malformed.
    """
    result_path = out_dir / "result.json"
    if not result_path.is_file():
        raise OutputParseError(f"no result.json found in {out_dir}")
    try:
        return Result.model_validate_json(result_path.read_text())
    except ValueError as exc:  # pydantic ValidationError is a ValueError
        raise OutputParseError(f"malformed result.json in {out_dir}: {exc}") from exc


def _artifacts(result: Result, kind: ArtifactKind) -> list:
    return [a for a in result.artifacts if a.kind == kind]


def load_pooled_embeddings(out_dir: Path, result: Result) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
    """Load pooled embeddings (one vector per record) keyed by record id.

    Raises:
        OutputParseError: If no pooled-embeddings artifact is present.
    """
    artifacts = _artifacts(result, ArtifactKind.POOLED_EMBEDDINGS)
    if not artifacts:
        raise OutputParseError("result declares no pooled_embeddings artifact")
    with np.load(out_dir / artifacts[0].path) as npz:
        return {key: npz[key] for key in npz.files}


def load_per_residue_embeddings(out_dir: Path, result: Result) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
    """Load per-residue embeddings keyed by record id (the artifact filename stem)."""
    out: dict[str, np.ndarray] = {}
    for artifact in _artifacts(result, ArtifactKind.PER_RESIDUE_EMBEDDINGS):
        out[Path(artifact.path).stem] = np.load(out_dir / artifact.path)
    if not out:
        raise OutputParseError("result declares no per_residue_embeddings artifacts")
    return out


def _read_csv_artifact(
    out_dir: Path,
    result: Result,
    kind: ArtifactKind,
    column_types: dict[str, type],
) -> list[dict[str, str | int | float | None]]:
    """Read a single CSV artifact, coercing numeric columns (blanks -> None).

    Raises:
        OutputParseError: If the result declares no artifact of the given kind.
    """
    artifacts = _artifacts(result, kind)
    if not artifacts:
        raise OutputParseError(f"result declares no {kind.value} artifact")
    rows: list[dict[str, str | int | float | None]] = []
    with (out_dir / artifacts[0].path).open(newline="") as handle:
        for raw_row in csv.DictReader(handle):
            row: dict[str, str | int | float | None] = {}
            for key, value in raw_row.items():
                caster = column_types.get(key, str)
                if caster is not str and value == "":
                    row[key] = None
                else:
                    row[key] = caster(value)
            rows.append(row)
    return rows


def read_likelihoods(out_dir: Path, result: Result) -> list[dict[str, str | int | float | None]]:
    """Read the likelihoods CSV, coercing known numeric columns."""
    return _read_csv_artifact(
        out_dir, result, ArtifactKind.LIKELIHOODS_CSV, _LIKELIHOOD_COLUMN_TYPES
    )


def read_generated(out_dir: Path, result: Result) -> list[FastaRecord]:
    """Load generated sequences from the ``generated_fasta`` artifact.

    Raises:
        OutputParseError: If no generated_fasta artifact is present.
    """
    artifacts = _artifacts(result, ArtifactKind.GENERATED_FASTA)
    if not artifacts:
        raise OutputParseError("result declares no generated_fasta artifact")
    return read_fasta(out_dir / artifacts[0].path)


def read_variant_scores(out_dir: Path, result: Result) -> list[dict[str, str | int | float | None]]:
    """Read the variant scores CSV (blank score => None for invalid rows)."""
    return _read_csv_artifact(out_dir, result, ArtifactKind.VARIANT_SCORES_CSV, _SCORE_COLUMN_TYPES)

1	"""Model registry: resolve model names/aliases to Docker image references.
2	
3	The registry is backed by a human-editable YAML file. The default registry
4	ships inside the package (``protlms/_data/models.yaml``); a custom file can be
5	supplied for testing or local overrides.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from importlib import resources
11	from pathlib import Path
12	
13	import yaml
14	from pydantic import BaseModel, field_validator
15	
16	from protlms.exceptions import ModelNotFoundError
17	
18	_DEFAULT_REGISTRY_RESOURCE = "_data/models.yaml"
19	
20	
21	class BuildSpec(BaseModel):
22	    """Build-only metadata for the publishing pipeline; ignored by the client."""
23	
24	    context: str
25	    args: dict[str, str] = {}
26	
27	
28	class ModelEntry(BaseModel):
29	    """One registry entry mapping a model to its image."""
30	
31	    name: str
32	    aliases: list[str] = []
33	    image: str
34	    digest: str | None = None
35	    model_family: str
36	    build: BuildSpec | None = None
37	
38	    @field_validator("digest")
39	    @classmethod
40	    def _validate_digest(cls, value: str | None) -> str | None:
41	        """Reject digests that are not ``sha256:`` references."""
42	        if value is not None and not value.startswith("sha256:"):
43	            raise ValueError(f"digest must start with 'sha256:', got {value!r}")
44	        return value
45	
46	    def pinned_ref(self) -> str:
47	        """Image reference to pull/run.
48	
49	        Returns ``<repo>@<digest>`` when a digest is set (reproducible), else the
50	        bare ``image`` tag (e.g. a locally-built image with no published digest).
51	        """
52	        if self.digest is None:
53	            return self.image
54	        return f"{self._strip_tag(self.image)}@{self.digest}"
55	
56	    @staticmethod
57	    def _strip_tag(image: str) -> str:
58	        """Drop a ``:tag`` from the final path segment of an image reference."""
59	        prefix, sep, last = image.rpartition("/")
60	        name = last.split(":", 1)[0]
61	        return f"{prefix}{sep}{name}" if sep else name
62	
63	
64	class Registry:
65	    """An in-memory model registry loaded from YAML."""
66	
67	    def __init__(self, entries: list[ModelEntry]) -> None:
68	        self._entries = entries
69	        self._by_key: dict[str, ModelEntry] = {}
70	        for entry in entries:
71	            for key in (entry.name, *entry.aliases):
72	                self._by_key[key] = entry
73	
74	    @classmethod
75	    def load(cls, path: Path | None = None) -> Registry:
76	        """Load a registry from YAML.
77	
78	        Args:
79	            path: A YAML file to load. If ``None``, the packaged default
80	                registry is used.
81	
82	        Returns:
83	            The loaded registry.
84	        """
85	        if path is None:
86	            text = resources.files("protlms").joinpath(_DEFAULT_REGISTRY_RESOURCE).read_text()
87	        else:
88	            text = Path(path).read_text()
89	        data = yaml.safe_load(text) or {}
90	        entries = [ModelEntry.model_validate(item) for item in data.get("models", [])]
91	        return cls(entries)
92	
93	    def resolve(self, name: str) -> ModelEntry:
94	        """Resolve a model name or alias to its registry entry.
95	
96	        Raises:
97	            ModelNotFoundError: If ``name`` matches no entry.
98	        """
99	        try:
100	            return self._by_key[name]
101	        except KeyError:
102	            known = ", ".join(sorted(e.name for e in self._entries))
103	            raise ModelNotFoundError(f"unknown model {name!r}; known models: {known}") from None
104	
105	    def list_models(self) -> list[ModelEntry]:
106	        """Return all registry entries in file order."""
107	        return list(self._entries)
108

aiTitle: Implement contacts capability for protein language models

content.text: Let me examine the reference entrypoint and a sample CASP14 PDB to understand what the container needs and what ground-truth data is available.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python
2	"""Contract-compliant entrypoint for the ESM2 model image.
3	
4	Implements the protlms container contract (see docs/CONTRACT.md) for the ESM2
5	masked protein language model via HuggingFace ``transformers``. Exposes the
6	``manifest``, ``embed``, ``likelihood``, and ``score`` subcommands plus a hidden
7	``_prefetch`` used at build time to bake weights into the image.
8	
9	This file is intentionally dependency-light at import time: ``torch`` and
10	``transformers`` are imported inside the functions that need them, so the pure
11	helpers can be unit-tested without the ML stack installed.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import argparse
17	import json
18	import os
19	import re
20	import sys
21	from pathlib import Path
22	
23	CONTRACT_VERSION = "0.3"
24	MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH = 1024
25	DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE = 8
26	DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT = os.environ.get("ESM2_CHECKPOINT", "esm2_t6_8M")
27	
28	_ID_SAFE = re.compile(r"[^A-Za-z0-9._-]")
29	_MUTANT_RE = re.compile(r"^([A-Za-z])(\d+)([A-Za-z])$")
30	
31	
32	# --- pure helpers (unit-testable without torch) ----------------------------
33	
34	
35	def resolve_hf_id(checkpoint: str) -> str:
36	    """Resolve a short ESM2 checkpoint name to a HuggingFace model id.
37	
38	    ``esm2_t6_8M`` -> ``facebook/esm2_t6_8M_UR50D``. A value already containing
39	    ``/`` is treated as a full HuggingFace id and returned unchanged.
40	    """
41	    if "/" in checkpoint:
42	        return checkpoint
43	    suffix = "" if checkpoint.endswith("_UR50D") else "_UR50D"
44	    return f"facebook/{checkpoint}{suffix}"
45	
46	
47	def sanitize_ids(ids: list[str]) -> list[str]:
48	    """Sanitize record ids for filenames/keys, de-duplicating collisions."""
49	    seen: dict[str, int] = {}
50	    out: list[str] = []
51	    for raw in ids:
52	        clean = _ID_SAFE.sub("_", raw) or "seq"
53	        if clean in seen:
54	            seen[clean] += 1
55	            clean = f"{clean}__{seen[clean]}"
56	        else:
57	            seen[clean] = 0
58	        out.append(clean)
59	    return out
60	
61	
62	def read_fasta(path: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
63	    """Parse a FASTA file into ``(id, sequence)`` tuples."""
64	    records: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
65	    header: str | None = None
66	    chunks: list[str] = []
67	    for raw in Path(path).read_text().splitlines():
68	        line = raw.strip()
69	        if not line:
70	            continue
71	        if line.startswith(">"):
72	            if header is not None:
73	                records.append((header, "".join(chunks).upper()))
74	            header = line[1:].split(maxsplit=1)[0] if line[1:].split() else line[1:]
75	            chunks = []
76	        else:
77	            chunks.append(line)
78	    if header is not None:
79	        records.append((header, "".join(chunks).upper()))
80	    return records
81	
82	
83	def perplexity_from_mean(mean_log_likelihood: float) -> float:
84	    """Pseudo-perplexity from a mean pseudo-log-likelihood."""
85	    import math
86	
87	    return math.exp(-mean_log_likelihood)
88	
89	
90	def parse_mutant(mutant: str) -> list[tuple[str, int, str]]:
91	    """Parse a mutation string like ``A24G`` or ``A24G:T56S`` (1-indexed)."""
92	    subs: list[tuple[str, int, str]] = []
93	    for token in mutant.split(":"):
94	        match = _MUTANT_RE.match(token.strip())
95	        if not match:
96	            raise ValueError(f"invalid mutation token {token!r}")
97	        subs.append((match.group(1).upper(), int(match.group(2)), match.group(3).upper()))
98	    return subs
99	
100	
101	def _valid_positions(group: list[dict], wt_seq: str) -> set[int]:
102	    """Collect in-range mutated positions across a WT group (for logit computation)."""
103	    positions: set[int] = set()
104	    for row in group:
105	        try:
106	            for _wt, pos, _mut in parse_mutant(row["mutant"]):
107	                if 1 <= pos <= len(wt_seq):
108	                    positions.add(pos)
109	        except ValueError:
110	            continue
111	    return positions
112	
113	
114	# --- contract I/O ----------------------------------------------------------
115	
116	
117	def write_result(output_dir: Path, payload: dict) -> None:
118	    """Write the ``result.json`` success summary."""
119	    (output_dir / "result.json").write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2))
120	
121	
122	def emit_error_and_exit(error_type: str, message: str, **details: str) -> None:
123	    """Write a structured ContainerError to stderr and exit non-zero."""
124	    error = {
125	        "contract_version": CONTRACT_VERSION,
126	        "error_type": error_type,
127	        "message": message,
128	        "details": {k: str(v) for k, v in details.items()},
129	    }
130	    print(json.dumps(error), file=sys.stderr)
131	    raise SystemExit(1)
132	
133	
134	# --- model helpers ---------------------------------------------------------
135	
136	
137	def pick_device(requested: str | None) -> str:
138	    """Choose the torch device, validating an explicit ``cuda`` request."""
139	    import torch
140	
141	    available = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
142	    if requested in (None, "auto"):
143	        return available
144	    if requested == "cuda" and available != "cuda":
145	        emit_error_and_exit("DeviceUnavailable", "cuda requested but no GPU is available")
146	    return requested
147	
148	
149	def load_model(device: str):  # noqa: ANN201 - returns (tokenizer, model)
150	    """Load the tokenizer and masked-LM model for the configured checkpoint."""
151	    import torch
152	    from transformers import AutoModelForMaskedLM, AutoTokenizer
153	
154	    hf_id = resolve_hf_id(DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT)
155	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(hf_id)
156	    model = AutoModelForMaskedLM.from_pretrained(hf_id, torch_dtype=torch.float32)
157	    model.eval().to(device)
158	    return tokenizer, model
159	
160	
161	def build_manifest() -> dict:
162	    """Build the manifest dict from the checkpoint's config."""
163	    from transformers import AutoConfig
164	
165	    config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(resolve_hf_id(DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT))
166	    return {
167	        "contract_version": CONTRACT_VERSION,
168	        "name": DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT,
169	        "version": "1.0.0",
170	        "description": f"ESM2 masked protein language model ({DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT}).",
171	        "model_family": "esm2",
172	        "capabilities": ["embed", "likelihood", "score"],
173	        "embedding_dim": int(config.hidden_size),
174	        "max_sequence_length": MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH,
175	        "pooling_modes": ["mean", "cls", "none"],
176	        "num_layers": int(config.num_hidden_layers),
177	        "min_gpu_memory_gb": None,
178	        "default_batch_size": DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE,
179	    }
180	
181	
182	def _truncate(seq: str, warnings: list[str], record_id: str) -> str:
183	    if len(seq) > MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH:
184	        warnings.append(f"sequence {record_id!r} truncated to {MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH} residues")
185	        return seq[:MAX_SEQUENCE_LENGTH]
186	    return seq
187	
188	
189	# --- subcommands -----------------------------------------------------------
190	
191	
192	def cmd_manifest(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
193	    print(json.dumps(build_manifest()))
194	
195	
196	def cmd_embed(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
197	    import torch
198	
199	    device = pick_device(args.device)
200	    tokenizer, model = load_model(device)
201	    layer = int(args.layers.split(",")[0])
202	    records = read_fasta(Path(args.input))
203	    ids = sanitize_ids([rid for rid, _ in records])
204	    output_dir = Path(args.output)
205	    warnings: list[str] = []
206	
207	    pooled: dict[str, object] = {}
208	    artifacts: list[dict] = []
209	    per_residue_dir = output_dir / "per_residue"
210	    if args.pooling == "none":
211	        per_residue_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
212	
213	    for clean_id, (rid, seq) in zip(ids, records, strict=True):
214	        seq = _truncate(seq, warnings, rid)
215	        residue, cls_vec = _embed_one(tokenizer, model, seq, layer, device)
216	        if args.pooling == "mean":
217	            pooled[clean_id] = residue.mean(axis=0)
218	        elif args.pooling == "cls":
219	            pooled[clean_id] = cls_vec
220	        else:  # none
221	            _save_npy(per_residue_dir / f"{clean_id}.npy", residue)
222	            artifacts.append(
223	                {
224	                    "path": f"per_residue/{clean_id}.npy",
225	                    "kind": "per_residue_embeddings",
226	                    "record_ids": [clean_id],
227	                    "shape": list(residue.shape),
228	                    "dtype": "float32",
229	                }
230	            )
231	
232	    if args.pooling in ("mean", "cls"):
233	        import numpy as np
234	
235	        np.savez(output_dir / "embeddings.npz", **pooled)
236	        artifacts.append(
237	            {
238	                "path": "embeddings.npz",
239	                "kind": "pooled_embeddings",
240	                "record_ids": ids,
241	                "shape": [len(ids), model.config.hidden_size],
242	                "dtype": "float32",
243	            }
244	        )
245	    _write_capability_result(output_dir, "embed", records, artifacts, warnings, args)
246	    del torch  # silence unused-import linters; torch is used transitively above
247	
248	
249	def _embed_one(tokenizer, model, seq: str, layer: int, device: str):  # noqa: ANN001, ANN202
250	    import numpy as np
251	    import torch
252	
253	    enc = tokenizer(seq, return_tensors="pt").to(device)
254	    use_amp = device == "cuda"
255	    with torch.no_grad(), torch.autocast(device_type="cuda", enabled=use_amp):
256	        out = model(**enc, output_hidden_states=True)
257	    hidden = out.hidden_states[layer][0].float().cpu().numpy()  # (T, D)
258	    residue = hidden[1 : 1 + len(seq)].astype(np.float32)  # strip <cls>/<eos>
259	    cls_vec = hidden[0].astype(np.float32)
260	    return residue, cls_vec
261	
262	
263	def cmd_likelihood(args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
264	    device = pick_device(args.device)
265	    tokenizer, model = load_model(device)
266	    batch_size = args.batch_size or DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE
267	    records = read_fasta(Path(args.input))
268	    ids = sanitize_ids([rid for rid, _ in records])
269	    output_dir = Path(args.output)
270	    warnings: list[str] = []
271	
272	    rows = ["record_id,seq_len,log_likelihood,mean_log_likelihood,perplexity"]
273	    for clean_id, (rid, seq) in zip(ids, records, strict=True):
274	        seq = _truncate(seq, warnings, rid)
275	        pll = _pseudo_log_likelihood(tokenizer, model, seq, batch_size, device)
276	        mean = pll / max(len(seq), 1)
277	        rows.append(f"{clean_id},{len(seq)},{pll:.6f},{mean:.6f},{perplexity_from_mean(mean):.6f}")
278	    (output_dir / "likelihoods.csv").write_text("\n".join(rows) + "\n")
279	
280	    artifacts = [{"path": "likelihoods.csv", "kind": "likelihoods_csv", "record_ids": ids}]
281	    _write_capability_result(output_dir, "likelihood", records, artifacts, warnings, args)
282	
283	
284	def _pseudo_log_likelihood(tokenizer, model, seq: str, batch_size: int, device: str) -> float:  # noqa: ANN001
285	    """Masked-marginal pseudo-log-likelihood: O(L) masked forward passes."""
286	    import torch
287	
288	    enc = tokenizer(seq, return_tensors="pt")
289	    input_ids = enc["input_ids"][0]  # (L+2,)
290	    positions = list(range(1, len(input_ids) - 1))  # residues only
291	    total = 0.0
292	    for start in range(0, len(positions), batch_size):
293	        chunk = positions[start : start + batch_size]
294	        batch = input_ids.repeat(len(chunk), 1)  # (b, L+2)
295	        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
296	            batch[row, pos] = tokenizer.mask_token_id
297	        with torch.no_grad():
298	            logits = model(input_ids=batch.to(device)).logits  # (b, L+2, V)
299	        log_probs = torch.log_softmax(logits.float(), dim=-1)
300	        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
301	            total += float(log_probs[row, pos, input_ids[pos]])
302	    return total
303	
304	
305	def cmd_prefetch(_args: argparse.Namespace) -> None:
306	    """Bake weights into the image at build time (populate the HF cache)."""
307	    from transformers import AutoModelForMaskedLM, AutoTokenizer
308	
309	    hf_id = resolve_hf_id(DEFAULT_CHECKPOINT)
310	    AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(hf_id)
311	    AutoModelForMaskedLM.from_pretrained(hf_id)
312	    print(f"prefetched {hf_id}")
313	
314	
315	def _masked_position_logprobs(tokenizer, model, seq, positions, batch_size, device):  # noqa: ANN001
316	    """Map each 1-indexed position to its masked log-softmax vector over the vocab."""
317	    import torch
318	
319	    input_ids = tokenizer(seq, return_tensors="pt")["input_ids"][0]
320	    ordered = sorted(positions)
321	    out: dict[int, object] = {}
322	    for start in range(0, len(ordered), batch_size):
323	        chunk = ordered[start : start + batch_size]
324	        batch = input_ids.repeat(len(chunk), 1)
325	        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
326	            batch[row, pos] = tokenizer.mask_token_id
327	        with torch.no_grad():
328	            logits = model(input_ids=batch.to(device)).logits
329	        log_probs = torch.log_softmax(logits.float(), dim=-1)
330	        for row, pos in enumerate(chunk):
331	            out[pos] = log_probs[row, pos].cpu().numpy()
332	    return out
333	
334	
335	def _wt_position_logprobs(tokenizer, model, seq, positions, device):  # noqa: ANN001
336	    """Map each 1-indexed position to its unmasked (WT-context) log-softmax vector."""
337	    import torch
338	
339	    enc = tokenizer(seq, return_tensors="pt").to(device)
340	    with torch.no_grad():
341	        logits = model(**enc).logits
342	    log_probs = torch.log_softmax(logits.float(), dim=-1)[0].cpu().numpy()
343	    return {pos: log_probs[pos] for pos in positions}
344	
345	
346	def _score_variant(mutant, wt_seq, logp_by_pos, tokenizer):  # noqa: ANN001
347	    """Return (score, n_mutations, error_message_or_None) for one variant."""
348	    try:
